48696982.title: Fintech Engineering Handbook
48696982.summary: The Fintech Engineering Handbook outlines patterns for trustworthy money systems, covering representation, precision, rounding, currency handling, FX rates, ledgers, audits, event sourcing and immutability.

48689028.title: Previewing GPT‑5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
48689028.summary: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol, a next-generation model with stronger coding, science, and cybersecurity capabilities, paired with its most advanced safety stack.

48696510.title: Beer CSS – Build material design in record time
48696510.summary: Beer CSS is a lightweight Material Design 3 framework that builds modern interfaces fast with no build steps and minimal footprint, ready to integrate with any JS framework.

48696585.title: DeepSeek open-sources inference optimizations with 60–85% faster generation [pdf]
48696585.summary: DeepSeek has open-sourced its inference optimizations, delivering 60–85% faster generation. The release highlights techniques for accelerating speculative decoding.

48697560.title: OpenRA
48697560.summary: OpenRA is an open-source project modernizing classic real-time strategy games like Red Alert, Command and Conquer, and Dune 2000, with playtests and a thriving community.

48668747.title: Linux on Older Hardware: The Complete Revival Guide
48668747.summary: The guide shows how to bring new life to aging PCs with Linux, testing lightweight distros, zram tuning, SSD upgrades, and browser tweaks, including hands-on results from a 2014 ThinkPad.

48697504.title: Nox Metals (YC S25) Is Hiring SWE
48697504.summary: Nox Metals, a YC-backed manufacturing software company, is hiring software engineers in Detroit to build an end-to-end platform that quotes orders, manages inventory, and connects shop-floor hardware.

48677564.title: Long Wave radio era set to end with switch-off
48677564.summary: The BBC is shutting down its long-wave radio service, ending its oldest broadcast era; the switch-off pushes audiences toward digital and alternative platforms.

48831384.title: Text art tools
48831384.summary: The piece surveys software and online tools for creating text art, from ASCII art generators to fonts and styles. It notes how these tools enable designers to craft decorative text and art for social posts and digital media.

48875377.title: Show HN: Ant – A JavaScript runtime and ecosystem
48875377.summary: Ant is a lightweight JavaScript runtime built from scratch with a Node-compatible API. It can run real npm packages and TypeScript from a compact 9 MB binary, and includes a VM-isolated sandbox and built-in Wasm support.

48854168.title: The 'Father of the Internet' is finally retiring
48854168.summary: Vinton Cerf, widely known as the Father of the Internet, is retiring from his role as Google's chief internet evangelist. The veteran technologist helped design the TCP/IP protocols that power the web. Cerf's departure closes a formative chapter in the history of digital connectivity.

48876505.title: Mesh LLM: distributed AI computing on iroh
48876505.summary: Mesh LLM pools GPUs across many machines and exposes a single OpenAI-compatible API, letting you run models locally, on peers, or split across nodes. Built on iroh’s peer-to-peer network with QUIC, it eliminates central servers and lets you control where your data and compute live.

48949551.title: Thanks HN for 15 years of support and helping me find my life's work
48949551.summary: Grateful for 15 years of support, the author thanks Hacker News for being a steady presence. They credit the community with helping them discover their life's work and steer their career. The note reflects on impact, gratitude, and a sense of purpose found online.

48945241.title: AWS: Inaccurate Estimated Billing Data – $1.7 billion
48945241.summary: AWS revealed inaccuracies in its estimated billing data, tied to about $1.7 billion. The flaw prompted remediation efforts and raised questions about billing data quality and cross-checks to prevent future errors.

48907063.title: Reviving a 15-year-old netbook with Arch Linux
48907063.summary: Simon Park revives a 15-year-old ASUS Eee PC 1000HE by installing Arch Linux 32 on its 32-bit Atom CPU. He details a step-by-step process: boot media, network setup, system clock, disk partitioning with a large swap, and finally installing Arch with GRUB.

48954851.title: Regressive JPEGs
48954851.summary: Regressive JPEGs explores how JPEGs can display progressively, with single low data first and multiple scans. It shows how to concatenate images or craft frames by controlling DC/AC data, using YCbCr subsampling, and create looping 'videos' in one image, noting browser limits and practicality.

48956688.title: LG monitors silently install software through Windows Update without consent
48956688.summary: Connecting some LG monitors to a Windows PC may automatically install software that promotes McAfee subscriptions, without user consent. The installation occurs via Windows Update. The report underscores concerns over bundled software and user privacy.

48956924.title: Why do AI company logos look like buttholes?
48956924.summary: VelvetShark humorously argues that many AI logos resemble buttholes due to circular gradients, central openings, and design drift. It traces a trend from OpenAI to others, explains pareidolia and risk-averse design, and suggests ways to break free with sharper shapes, negative space, or bold color.

48923929.title: Funny item co-occurrences in 3.2M Instacart orders
48923929.summary: Roger Dickey analyzes 3.2 million Instacart orders to find funny item co-occurrences, showing most pairings are rare yet plentiful. He ranks co-occurrences by lift and adds a humor score using the GS1 GPC brick system, arguing this sharpens what's funny. Small carts reveal punchlines.

48956949.title: What AI did to stackoverflow in a graph
48956949.summary: A data graph shows how AI reshaped Stack Overflow over time, with shifts in topics and activity. It highlights a surge in AI-related questions and new answer patterns as developers lean on AI tools. The visualization underlines AI's growing influence on the site's community.
